signals.evidence: Vienna onsite (Teil 1+2) with CPO Matthias Flandorfer, Pölki, Karin Eppich, Melissa Koller + Zycus (Amit/Dirk); commercial + agentic-AI walkthrough. 'you're already in the shortlist ... we hope we can close it nearly next time'
Pölki pricing call: reviewed revised numbers + new transaction-based Merlin Intake model; 'I have to calculate it internally ... a good definition what is one transaction'
Zycus offered CEO/CIO engagement; Pölki's boss declined: 'he don't want to get his CIO at the moment ... helpful maybe in the next step'
Coupa near price-parity (475K vs Zycus 448K); Post bent its own process for Coupa. Zycus ahead on S2C+AI, rivals slight edge on P2P usability; Tronkeon a lower-cost bolt-on threat (24 Jun)
CIO SAP-integration effort (170–300 man-days) unresolved; IT resources diverted to Turkey S/4HANA. 'we cannot run it ... if not even a minimum of these IT resources is available'
No quantified case; high existing digitalization = 'no real sense of urgency'; boss 'only wants a new business case with the best prices we get from market'; must fight other departments for budget
'I can't tell you now an exact timeline. Nobody can tell you that' — two paths: start S2C Q4 2026 OR slip to mid/end 2027; P2P not before end-2027/2028
Forecast Best Case→Upside Key Deal (22 Jun); Amount 650K→448K (25 Jun, -31%); CloseDate 30 Jun→23 Jul
Pölki active champion (drives pricing, escalates internally, engages controlling) + Eppich technical; but admits 'wir werden auch ein bissl hingehalten' (we're being held back) by own leadership
Buyer's 'significant interest in the Merlin agentic AI front'; Zycus positioned outcome-agents vs point-agents, leader in all 4 analyst quadrants, native suite vs bolt-on

top_rationale: Genuinely hot, late-shortlist RFP: a CPO-level onsite (1 Jul) and a Pölki pricing call (7 Jul) sit on top of ~5 buyer sessions in the prior fortnight — engagement is fresh and two-way.
Zycus is the technical/price front-runner (448K vs Coupa 475K; buyer-acknowledged S2C + agentic-AI edge) with two working champions, Pölki (commercial) and Eppich (technical).
But it is NOT selected — 70 days in Shortlisted, buyer still comparing, champion openly 'being held back' by leadership.

Three hard blockers gate any 2026 close: an unresolved CIO SAP-integration objection (170–300 man-days), an unbuilt business case with no internal urgency, and no direct economic-buyer (CFO) engagement.
CRM conviction is slipping under the activity: forecast downgraded, amount cut 31%, close pushed to 23 Jul — a date the buyer themselves says may slip to 2027.

Coverage full: verbatim transcripts for the two decisive recent calls (1 & 7 Jul) plus SF facts, contact roles, activity trail and prior record; older Avoma transcripts empty but covered by next-step trail.

drivers: ✅ Multi-threaded senior access: DIRECT CPO (Flandorfer) face time at the 1 Jul Vienna onsite, plus dual working champions — Pölki negotiating commercials, Eppich validating tech.
✅ Differentiated + price-competitive front-runner: 448K vs Coupa 475K, buyer-acknowledged Source-to-Contract and agentic-AI (Merlin/ANA) edge, native-suite story landing.

⚠️ No selection despite ~70 days Shortlisted; buyer still benchmarking ('a new business case with the best prices we get from market'). ► Press Pölki for a written vendor-of-choice / order-of-preference signal before any further concession.

⚠️ CIO SAP-integration blocker (170–300 man-days) unresolved and IT diverted to Turkey S/4HANA; boss declined CIO/CEO exec engagement 'at the moment'. ► Land the AI SAP-mapping demo with Post IT to kill the effort objection and earn the IT green light.
⚠️ Business case unbuilt — buyer admits no urgency given high existing digitalization. ► Co-build the ROI/TCO model Zycus offered so Pölki can win the digital-roadmap board budget fight.

⚠️ Conviction slipping: forecast Best Case→Upside, amount 650K→448K, close pushed to 23 Jul which the buyer signals may slip to 2027. ► Reset the close to the realistic S2C-first timeline and correct the forecast in SFDC.
win.focus_now: Convert the intense pricing engagement into a written vendor-of-choice commitment from Pölki AND neutralize the CIO SAP-integration blocker — without both, the 23 Jul close is not real.
win.ceiling_applied: Shortlisted → RFP-round ceiling ≤60 (§5.1). Strong raw signals (anchor 50 + rubric + engagement pulse) reached the cap; no §5.5 stage-reality override (no MSA/SOW/legal redline, no buyer-confirmed selection), so 60 binds.

drivers: ✅ Fresh, dense two-way cadence: CPO-level Vienna onsite (1 Jul) + Pölki pricing negotiation (7 Jul) + ~5 buyer sessions in the prior fortnight — last real engagement just 2 days ago.
✅ Live commercial motion: transaction-based Merlin pricing, 7-yr S2C term (2-yr price guarantee), buyer actively requesting a refreshed implementation quote and business-case support.
✅ Rich, forward next-step trail (POC → onsite → pricing rounds) with continuous logged activity.

⚠️ No firm next session booked — 7 Jul closed on a loose 'catch up tomorrow/Thursday'; the buyer's next move is internal and undated (business case → digital-roadmap board). ► Lock a dated readout this week for the transaction model + joint business case.
⚠️ Forecast downgraded (Best Case→Upside) and close pushed to 23 Jul while the buyer floats a 2027 slip. ► Pin the digital-roadmap board date with Pölki so activity converts into a decision.
⚠️ Motion is somewhat circular — many pricing rounds, no advance to selection; champion 'being held back' by leadership. ► Tie the next commercial concession to a selection/shortlist-winner signal.
mom.focus_now: Bank the momentum by locking a dated next session and pinning the digital-roadmap board date — the deal is moving hard but has not yet advanced to selection.

prospect_requirements.item: Clear written definition of 'a transaction' for the Merlin Intake consumption model
Draft revised pricing sheet (with transaction caps/volumes) for buyer review
Refreshed implementation-cost proposal on current scope (prior quote ~3 months old)

zycus_commitments.item: Send the extra pricing spreadsheet with the revised numbers
zycus_commitments.grounding_quote: I will send you the extra spreadsheet with the numbers right after this meeting

zycus_commitments.item: Deliver the definition of a transaction
zycus_commitments.grounding_quote: we need to provide you with what is a transaction which we can do tomorrow by tomorrow evening

zycus_commitments.item: Co-build a joint business case from the buyer's numbers (cost-savings/ROI)
zycus_commitments.grounding_quote: we've got an internal team that can build and help build business case ... with your numbers

zycus_commitments.item: Provide a new implementation proposal with EY partner
zycus_commitments.grounding_quote: we will get a new proposal for implementation as well

waiting_on_buyer.item: Pölki to validate the ~18,000-transaction volume with his team and revert
Date of the digital-roadmap board where the project is ranked / scheduled
IT/CIO position (green light) on the SAP-integration effort

best_practices.item: Run the AI SAP-mapping demo for Post IT to neutralize the CIO's integration-effort objection
best_practices.rank_reason: Highest — buyer-voiced doubt about execution feasibility; the unresolved IT blocker is the single gate to any 2026 close

best_practices.item: Co-build and validate the ROI/TCO business case to arm Pölki for the digital-roadmap board
best_practices.rank_reason: No quantified case = no budget urgency (buyer: high digitalization, 'no real sense of urgency'); required to win the internal budget fight

best_practices.item: Secure a written vendor-of-choice / order-of-preference signal from Pölki (advance Shortlisted → Vendor Selected)
best_practices.rank_reason: Converts strong technical/price preference into a defensible selection and unblocks the next stage-gate

best_practices.item: Multi-thread to CFO Barbara Potisk-Eibensteiner (economic buyer) on the Anna-event variable-cost concern
best_practices.rank_reason: Economic buyer named in MEDDPICC but not directly engaged; her cost-structure concern must clear for budget approval

sum.headline: Buyer (Pölki) reviewed Zycus's revised pricing and the new transaction-based Merlin Intake model on 7 Jul, and will validate the transaction volumes with his team before reverting.
supporting: Zycus committed to send the draft pricing sheet plus a clear 'what is a transaction' definition (by 8 Jul) and to prepare a joint business case and a refreshed implementation quote.
Gain tempered by risk: buyer's leadership again declined direct CIO/CEO exec engagement 'at the moment', keeping the gating SAP-integration sign-off internal — the chief threat to the 23 Jul close.
sum.as_of_note: Strict 24h (8 Jul) was quiet; surfaced under the 48h safety-net — the 7 Jul pricing call is the last real, detailed step.
